Name: Azure - Deploy Azure App using config-zip
Description: Deploy an Azure App Service (Web App or Function App) using the `config-zip` option.  This template is compatible with deploying to Azure App Services that are on the `Flexible Consumption Plan`.

Parameters.Name: Template.Azure.Account
Parameters.Label: Azure Account
Parameters.HelpText: Choose the Azure account to use when executing this template.

Parameters.Name: Template.Azure.ResourceGroup.Name
Parameters.Label: Azure Resource Group Name
Parameters.HelpText: The name of the Azure Resource Group to deploy the App Service to.

Parameters.Name: Template.Azure.Service.Name
Parameters.Label: Azure App Service Name
Parameters.HelpText: The name of the Azure App Service (web app or function app) to deploy.

Parameters.Name: Template.Azure.Service.Type
Parameters.Label: Azure App Service Type
Parameters.HelpText: Select the type of App Service you want to deploy.

Parameters.Name: Template.Azure.Slot.Name
Parameters.Label: Slot Name
Parameters.HelpText: (Optional) Name of the slot to deploy to.  Leave blank if you're not using slot capabilities.

Parameters.Name: Template.Replace.VariablesInFiles.Pattern
Parameters.Label: Variable replacement in files pattern
Parameters.HelpText: This template has the feature of [Substitute Variables in Templates](https://octopus.com/docs/projects/steps/configuration-features/substitute-variables-in-templates) enabled, giving you the ability to replace Octopus Variable place holders in files.  You can specify more than one file or pattern, newline separated.

Parameters.Name: Template.Replace.StructuredConfigurationVariables.Pattern
Parameters.Label: Structured configuration variable replacement
Parameters.HelpText: This template has the [Structured Configuration Variables](https://octopus.com/docs/projects/steps/configuration-features/structured-configuration-variables-feature) feature enabled giving you the ability to replace existing values within files.  You can specify more than one file or pattern, newline separated.
